title: Grüezi
desc: Full circle — now you're the one who welcomes

sn.s: Am Bahnhof gseh ich öpper mit eme grosse Koffer.
sn.e: At the station I see someone with a big suitcase.

sn.s: Si luegt ume, verlore, grad wie ich domals.
sn.e: She looks around, lost, just like me back then.

sn.s: Si haltet en Zettel mit nere Adress i de Hand.
sn.e: She holds a slip of paper with an address in her hand.

sn.s: "Excuse me," seit si, "do you speak English?"
sn.e: "Excuse me," she says, "do you speak English?"

sn.s: Ich lächle, well ich gnau weiss, wie si sich fühlt.
sn.e: I smile, because I know exactly how she feels.

sn.s: "Ja," säg ich. "Aber probier's mal mit Grüezi."
sn.e: "Yes," I say. "But try it with Grüezi."

sn.s: "Genau! Das heisst Hallo. Das isch de Aafang."
sn.e: "Exactly! That means hello. That's the beginning."

sn.s: Ich zeig ere de Wäg und gib ere es paar Wörtli mit.
sn.e: I show her the way and give her a few little words to take along.

sn.s: "Es wird nöd eifach," säg ich, "aber es lohnt sich."
sn.e: "It won't be easy," I say, "but it's worth it."
"Welcome to Lucerne. It'll turn out fine here."

sn.s: Si gaht witer, und ich lue ere nache.
sn.e: She walks on, and I watch her go.

sn.s: Wo ich aagfange han, fangt jetz öpper anders aa. Und ich bi dehei.
sn.e: Where I once began, someone else now begins. And I am home.
